headline: Models nail tech rally while Claude's XLE oil bet crashes spectacularly
summary: AI models showed strong consensus on tech and broad market gains, with SPY and QQQ predictions proving remarkably accurate across the board. Claude's high-confidence XLE call expecting $98.50 catastrophically missed as the energy ETF plummeted to $55.70, despite reasoning about Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

consensus_picks.note: All five models correctly predicted SPY's rise to $737.62, showing strong consensus on broad market strength.
Four models unanimously called QQQ's surge to $711.23, capturing the tech sector's outperformance.
Three models incorrectly predicted TSLA would fall, but it rallied to $428.35 instead.

best_call.summary: Grok's 70% confidence NVDA call perfectly nailed the $215.20 close with impressive precision on AI hardware buzz.

worst_call.summary: Claude's 78% confidence XLE prediction expecting $98.50 crashed as the ETF inexplicably fell to $55.70, a stunning 43% miss.
